title: Mixture-of-Schedulers: An Adaptive Scheduling Agent as a Learned Router for Expert Policies
authors: Not specified in the provided text
abstract: This paper introduces ASA (Adaptive Scheduling Agent), a novel approach to operating system scheduling that dynamically selects the best scheduler from a portfolio of expert schedulers based on current system conditions, rather than designing a single optimal scheduler.

methodology.approach: A paradigm shift from designing a single optimal policy to dynamically selecting the best policy from a portfolio of experts
methodology.implementation: ASA uses an intelligent agent to select the most suitable scheduler at runtime
methodology.inspiration: The approach is inspired by sched_ext which provides a series of expert schedulers

key_results.performance: ASA consistently outperforms the default Linux scheduler (EEVDF)
key_results.superior_results: ASA achieves superior results in 86.4% of test scenarios
key_results.near_optimal: ASA's selections are near-optimal, ranking among the top three schedulers in 78.6% of all scenarios

evaluation.benchmark: A novel benchmark focused on user-experience metrics
